[[Draft:Sandbox|sandbox]] to experiment with page editing.|imageright=[[File:Sandbox.svg|52px]]}} *'''[[Wikipedia:About|About Wikipedia]]''' – general introduction for visitors to Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Administration|Administration]] – discuses both the non-human administrative structure of Wikipedia, as well as its human components. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Overview|FAQ]] – the most common questions about Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Readers|Readers' FAQ]] – concerns and questions readers may have. *[[Wikipedia:Advice for parents|Advice for parents]] – information for parents and legal guardians. * '''[[Wikipedia:Contact us|Contact Wikipedia]]''' – how to get in touch with Wikipedia directly. *'''[[Wikipedia:General disclaimer|General disclaimer]]''' – there is no guarantee of validity and reliability. **[[Wikipedia:Content disclaimer|Content disclaimer]] – Wikipedia is not censored. **[[Wikipedia:Legal disclaimer|Legal disclaimer]] – Wikipedia does not give legal opinions. **[[Wikipedia:Medical disclaimer|Medical disclaimer]] – Wikipedia does not give medical advice. **[[Wikipedia:Risk disclaimer|Risk disclaimer]] – use Wikipedia at your own risk. *'''[[Wikipedia:Policies and guidelines|Policies & guidelines]]''' – community standards set forth by ''[[Wikipedia community|Wikipedians]]''. *'''[[Wikipedia:Principles|Principles]]''' – a listing of fundamental principles by which Wikipedia operates. **[[User:Jimbo Wales/Statement of principles|Statement of principles]] – by founder ''[[Jimmy Wales]]'' and Wikipedians. **[[Wikipedia:Wikipedia in brief|Wikipedia in brief]] – Wikipedia aims to be neutral, verifiable and factual. *[[Wikipedia:Researching with Wikipedia|Researching]] – Wikipedia can be a great tool for learning, however{{nbsp}}... *[[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not|What Wikipedia is not]] – there are certain things that Wikipedia is not. *[[Wikipedia:Questions|Where to ask questions]] – find the right place to ask a question. *[[Wikipedia:Requests|Where to make requests]] – find the right place to make a request. *[[Wikipedia:Who writes Wikipedia|Who writes Wikipedia]] – {{underline|You do!}} {{mono|Just do it!}} {{strong|{{em|[[Wikipedia:Be bold|Be bold!]]}}}} ===Contributor information=== {{Portal|Help}} *'''[[Wikipedia:Contributing to Wikipedia|Contributing to Wikipedia]]''' – the main page that provides information, links, videos and other resources on the basics needed to comprehend, comment on, and contribute to Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Core content policies|Core content policies]] – Wikipedia's content is governed by three principal core content policies. **[[Wikipedia:Simplified ruleset|Simplified rule-set]] – some basic aspect of Wikipedia norms and practices. **[[Wikipedia:Simplified Manual of Style|Simplified Manual of Style]] – the basics about commonly used style guidelines. *'''[[Wikipedia:Welcoming committee/Welcome to Wikipedia|Welcome to Wikipedia]]''' – portal style page to get you started. **[[Help:Getting started|Getting started]] – small listing of pages dealing with the basics. **[[Wikipedia:New contributors' help page|New contributors' help page]] – what would you like to do? **[[Wikipedia:Avoiding common mistakes|Common mistakes]] – a few common mistakes {{mono|''y'all''}} should try to avoid. **[[Wikipedia:Plain and simple|Plain and simple overview]] – all about this amazing project Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:A Primer for newcomers|Primer for newcomers]] – blunt introduction intended to help newcomers. **[[Wikipedia:Learning the ropes|Learning the ropes]] – highlights the resources to help you help Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Help hub|Help hub]] – a page that has links to useful directories. *'''[[Wikipedia:Why create an account?|Why create an account]]''' – don't need to be registered to edit, however it does provide additional features. **[[Wikipedia:Username policy|Choosing a username]] – do not choose names which may be offensive, misleading, disruptive, or promotional. **[[Help:Logging in|How to log in]] – If you are not logged in your edits are labelled in page history with your IP address. *'''[[Wikipedia:Your first article|Your first article]]''' – guide to starting your first encyclopedia article. **[[Wikipedia:Annotated article|Annotated article]] – is a well-constructed sample article, with annotations. ***'''[[Wikipedia:Article wizard|Article wizard]]''' – will walk you through the process of submitting a new article. ****[[Wikipedia:Creation and usage of media files|Creation and usage of media files]] – only logged in users can upload files. *****[[Wikipedia:File Upload Wizard|Upload Wizard]] – will walk you through the process of submitting media. *'''[[Wikipedia:FAQ|Frequently Asked Questions]]''' – common questions about using and contributing. **[[Wikipedia:Editorial oversight and control|Editorial oversight and control]]- we have tens of thousands of editors, from expert scholars to casual readers. **[[Wikipedia:How to help|How to help]] – what anyone can do to contribute. *[[Wikipedia:Instructional material|More instructional material]] – provides links to instructional material useful for users. **[[Wikipedia:Trifecta|Trifecta]] – ultra fast overview of foundational principles related to policies and guidelines. *[[Wikipedia:Ten things you may not know about Wikipedia|Things you may not know about Wikipedia]] – insights specifically targeted at people who have limited experience. *'''[[Wikipedia:Tip of the day|Tip of the day]]''' – provides "very useful" advice daily on how to use or develop Wikipedia more effectively. **See also '''[[Wikipedia:Tips]]''', the complete library of tips arranged by subject. * [[Wikipedia:User page design center|User page design center]] – where you will find all the resources for developing your user page. '''Enjoy!''' {{Back to contents}} ===Frequently asked questions===

*'''[[Wikipedia:FAQ index|FAQ main page]]''' – questions about using and contributing. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Administration|Administration]] – answers some questions related to Administrators. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Article subjects|Article subjects]] – what to do about a specific articles. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Categories|Categories]] – about using Wikipedia's categories. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Contributing|Contributing]] – answers to questions commonly asked by contributors. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Copyright|Copyright]] – four most commonly asked questions about copyright. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Editing|Editing]] – answers the most common questions about editing. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Forking|Forking]] – how do I download and use Wikipedia content. **[[Wikipedia:IRC|IRC (live chat)]] – about "chat rooms" – real-time discussions. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Organizations|Organizations]] – editing without displaying a conflict of interest. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Problems|Problems]] – solving problems you may encounter when browsing or editing. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Readers|Readers]] – addresses concerns and questions readers may have. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Schools|Schools]] – questions teachers, librarians and administrators might have. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Technical|Technical]] – answers some questions related to the technical workings. **([[Wikipedia:FAQ/Miscellaneous|Miscellaneous]]) – questions that do not fit into any of the others above. ===How to pages===

tab. **[[Wikipedia:Tutorial/Formatting|Formatting]] – the basics on coding. **[[Wikipedia:Tutorial/Wikipedia links|Links]] – the basics on internal links. **[[Wikipedia:Tutorial/Citing ''[[Wikipedia:IRC|freenode IRC servers]]'', you'll first need to. *''' [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/User guide|VisualEditor user guide]]''' – a guide that gives illustrated, step-by-step instructions about [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor|VisualEditor]] features. ===Introductions=== ====Wiki Markup==== *'''[[Help:Introduction|Main introduction]]''' – quick overview of what Wikipedia is all about. * [[Help:Introduction to editing with Wiki Markup/1|Editing]] – the basic pages on how to contribute to Wikipedia. * [[Help:Introduction to uploading images/1|Images]] – introduction to adding images to Wikipedia. * [[Help:Introduction to the Manual of Style|Manual of Style]] – introduction to the style guide for articles. * [[Wikipedia:Media help|Media]] – how to add media to Wikipedia. * [[Help:Introduction to navigating Wikipedia/1|Navigating]] – Wikipedia is a big place. * [[Help:Introduction to policies and guidelines|Policies]] – how to apply policies and guidelines. * [[Help:Introduction to referencing/1|Sourcing]] – why references are so important. * [[Help:Table/Introduction to tables|Tables]] – how an where tables are used and how to make them. * [[Help:Introduction to talk pages|Talk pages]] – how to communicate within Wikipedia. ====VisualEditor==== *[[Help:Introduction to editing (VE)/1|Editing with VisualEditor]] – a five part introduction to editing with VisualEditor. Opening the editor. Toolbar basics. Links and Wikilinks. Saving your changes. Summary * [[Help:Introduction to referencing (VE)/1|Referencing with VisualEditor]] – a five part introduction to referencing. Verifiability. Inline citations. RefToolbar. Reliable sources. Summary. * [[Help:Introduction to images (VE)/1|Uploading images with VisualEditor]] – a six part guide on uploading images. Introduction. Free content. Non-free content. Wikimedia Commons. Using an image. Summary. ===Training modules=== *[[Wikipedia:Training/For students|Training for students]] – four-part orientation intended for students doing ''[[Wikipedia:Assignments for student editors|Wikipedia assignments for class]]''. *[[Wikipedia:Training/For educators|Training for educators]] – four-part orientation for professors and other educators running assignments for class. *[[Wikipedia:Training/For Ambassadors|Training for Wikipedia Ambassadors]] – four-part orientation for ''[[Wikipedia:Ambassadors|Campus and Online Ambassadors]]''. * [[Wikipedia:Instructional material/MediaWiki training videos|MediaWiki training]] – learn about editing and formatting content using ''[[MediaWiki]]'', the software that powers Wikipedia. * [[Wikipedia:The Wikipedia Adventure|The Wikipedia Adventure]] – Wikipedia is not a game, but learning it should still be fun. *[[Help:Guided tours|Help Guided tours]] – providing tooltip-like tours of the Wikipedia experience. {{Back to contents}} ===The Missing Manual ===
{| style="background:transparent; width:50%;" |- ! colspan="2" style="background-color:transparent; font-size:125%" | bgcolor=transparent type=fulltext prefix=Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual break=yes width=25 searchbuttonlabel=Search placeholder=The Missing Manual |}
*'''[[Help: Wikipedia: The Missing Manual|The Missing Manual]]''' – comprehensive how-to guide (book) that explains everything about contributing for novice to expert editors. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Introduction|Introduction]] – originally written in 2008 by John Broughto, the Manual has since been expanded and updated by many others. ====Part I – Editing and Creating Articles==== *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Editing for the First Time|First edit]] – explains what you see when you look at an article in Wikipedia's editing window and how to practice. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Documenting Your Sources|Sourcing]] – you will need to learn some technical matters. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Setting Up Your Account and Personal Workspace|Account setup & personal space]] – having an account actually protects your privacy better than editing while logged out. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Creating a New Article|Creating articles]] – get a much better sense of what articles in Wikipedia should be like. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Who Did What: Page Histories and Reverting|Page history & reverting]] – as an editor you're likely to want to see what other editors do to articles you've edited. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Monitoring Changes|Monitoring changes]] – experienced editors monitor articles they've edited. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Editing, Creating, and Maintaining Articles/Dealing with Vandalism and Spam|Vandalism & spam]] – explains in detail what you, a Wikipedia editor, can do in terms of spotting and fixing vandalism and spam. ====Part II – Collaborating with Other Editors==== *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Collaborating with Other Editors/Communicating with Your Fellow Editors|Communicating with others]] – you will need to know how to use the pages where editors interact and collaborate with each other. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Collaborating with Other Editors/WikiProjects and Other Group Efforts|WikiProjects]] – many editors at Wikipedia work together in groups, formal or informal. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Collaborating with Other Editors/Resolving Content Disputes|Content disputes]] – if you find yourself involved in a content dispute{{nbsp}}... *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Collaborating with Other Editors/Handling Incivility and Personal Attacks|Incivility]] – shows you helpful ways to respond to incivility and personal attacks directed against you or other editors. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Collaborating with Other Editors/Lending Other Editors a Hand|Helping others]] – shows you all the places and ways you can lend other editors a hand. ====Part III – Formatting and Illustrating Articles==== *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Formatting and Illustrating Articles/Article Sections and Tables of Contents|Sections]] – shows you how to effectively use sections in an article. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Formatting and Illustrating Articles/Creating Lists and Tables|Lists & tables]] – shows you how to create and edit both lists and tables. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Formatting and Illustrating Articles/Adding Images|Images]] – shows you how to place an image in an article, after you or someone else has uploaded it. ====Part IV – Building a Stronger Encyclopedia==== *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Building a Stronger Encyclopedia/Getting Readers to the Right Article: Naming, Redirects, and Disambiguation|Naming, redirects & disambiguation]] – helping people to navigate Wikipedia. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Building a Stronger Encyclopedia/Categorizing Articles|Categorizing]] – adding categories to articles is easy. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Building a Stronger Encyclopedia/Better Articles: A Systematic Approach|Better articles]] – serve as a detailed checklist for improving articles. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Building a Stronger Encyclopedia/Deleting Existing Articles|Deletions]] – some editors abuse the privilege, creating nonsense articles, attack articles, or promotional articles. ====Part V – Customizing Wikipedia==== *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Customizing Wikipedia/Customizing with Preferences|Personal preferences]] – "My Preferences" is where you can change a number of settings that control how pages look and function. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Customizing Wikipedia/Easier Editing with JavaScript|JavaScript]] – you can customize Wikipedia in ways that make your editing easier with scripts. ====Part VI – Appendices==== *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Appendixes/A Tour of the Wikipedia Page|Wikipedia Pages]] – when you're registered, and logged into Wikipedia, you'll see links in a number of places. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Appendixes/Reader's Guide to Wikipedia|Reader's guide]] – background on what Wikipedia is and how to get the most out. *[[Help:Wikipedia: The Missing Manual/Appendixes/Learning More|Learning more]] – shows you the myriad places you can go, both inside and outside Wikipedia. {{Back to contents}} ===Interactive assistance (help forums)=== {{Back to contents}} ====Questions about Wikipedia==== {{Help desk}} {| align="center" width="100%" cellspacing="4" style="clear:both; background: transparent; text-align:center; font-size:85%; line-height:1.2;" |- |''[[Wikipedia:Help desk/How to answer|Replying to help requests]] – contains guidelines for users who respond to questions about how to use or edit posed by other users.'' |} * '''[[Wikipedia:Help desk|Help desk]]''' – the "main page" for asking questions about how to use or edit Wikipedia. * '''[[Wikipedia:Teahouse|Teahouse]]''' – a "very friendly place" for new editors to become accustomed to and ask questions about editing. ====General knowledge questions==== {| align="center" width="100%" cellspacing="4" style="clear:both; background: transparent; text-align:center; font-size:85%; line-height:1.2;" |- |''[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Guidelines|Replying to general knowledge questions]] – contains guidelines for users who respond to general knowledge questions posed by other users.'' '''''Note:''' legal or medical responses are prohibited. See Wikipedia's [[Wikipedia:Legal disclaimer|Legal disclaimer]] and [[Wikipedia:Medical disclaimer|Medical disclaimer]]''. |} *'''[[Wikipedia:Reference desk|Reference desks]]''' – you can ask questions about any topic at the specific pages listed below. **[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Computing|Computing]] – to ask about computing, information technology, electronics, software and hardware. **[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Entertainment|Entertainment]] – to ask about sports, popular culture, movies, music, video games, and TV shows. **[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Humanities|Humanities]] – to ask about history, politics, literature, religion, philosophy, law, finance, economics, art, and society. **[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Language|Language]] – to ask about spelling, grammar, word etymology, language usage, and translations. **[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Mathematics|Mathematics]] – to ask about mathematics, geometry, probability, and statistics. **[[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Science|Science]] – to ask about biology, chemistry, physics, medicine, geology, engineering and technology. **([[Wikipedia:Reference desk/Miscellaneous|Miscellaneous]]) – to ask about anything that is not listed above. ====Specific help and mediation==== *'''[[Wikipedia:Noticeboard|Noticeboards]]''' – Wikipedia noticeboards are pages where editors can ask questions and request assistance from people who are familiar with the policies and guidelines covered by each individual board. *[[Wikipedia:Administrators' noticeboard|Administrators]] – for posting information and issues that affect administrators. **[[Wikipedia:Administrators' noticeboard/Edit warring|Edit warring]] – for reporting active ''[[Wikipedia:Edit warring|edit warriors]]'' and recent violations of the ''[[WP:3RR|three-revert rule]]''. **[[Wikipedia:Administrators' noticeboard/Incidents|Incidents]] – for reporting and discussing incidents that require the intervention of administrators and experienced editors. **[[Wikipedia:Administrator intervention against vandalism|Vandalism]] – for reporting about obvious and persistent ''[[Wikipedia:Vandalism|vandals]]'' and ''[[Wikipedia:Spam|spammers]]''. *'''[[Wikipedia:Dispute resolution requests|Dispute resolution]]''' – provides a central compilation of the boards listed below to help resolves conflicts. **[[Wikipedia:Dispute resolution requests/Third Opinion|Third opinion]] – for disputes between two editors to receive an outside opinion. **[[Wikipedia:Dispute resolution requests/RfC|Requests for comment]] – the place to go to get outside input on issues from a broad number of users. **[[Wikipedia:Dispute resolution noticeboard|Dispute resolution noticeboard]] – used as a "first step" in solving content issues. **[[Wikipedia:Dispute resolution requests/ArbCom|Arbitration]] – the "last resort" for conduct issues when all other avenues are exhausted, issues binding rulings. *[[Wikipedia:Conflict of interest/Noticeboard|Conflict of interest]] – for determining whether a specific editor has a ''[[Wikipedia:Conflict of interest|conflict of interest]]''. *[[Wikipedia:External links/Noticeboard|External links]] – reporting possible breaches of the ''[[WP:EL|external links]]'' policy. *[[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view/Noticeboard|Neutrality]] – for reporting issues regarding whether article content is compliant with the ''[[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|Neutral Point of View]]'' policy. *[[Wikipedia:No original research/Noticeboard|Original research]] – for requesting input on possible problems of ''[[WP:OR|original research]]''. *[[Wikipedia:Reliable sources/Noticeboard|Sources]] – for posting questions regarding whether particular ''[[WP:RS|sources are reliable]]'' in context. *[[Wikipedia:Requested moves|Page moves]] – a process for requesting the retitling of an article, template, or project page. ====Technical issues==== *'''[[Wikipedia:Village pump|Village pump]]''' – main directory divided into five boards by topic (as seen below), to discuss the technical issues, policies, and operations of Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Village pump (policy)|Policy]] – to discuss changes to existing and proposed policies. **[[Wikipedia:Village pump (proposals)|Proposals]] – to discuss new proposals that are not policy-related. **[[Wikipedia:Village pump (technical)|Technical]] – to discuss technical issues. For wiki software bug reports, use Bugzilla **[[Wikipedia:Village pump (idea lab)|Idea lab]] – to discuss ideas before proposing them to the community and attempt to find solutions to common issues. **([[Wikipedia:Village pump (miscellaneous)|Miscellaneous]]) – to post messages that do not fit into any other categories listed above. ====Other ways to get help==== *'''[[Wikipedia:Special services|Special services]]''' – a section on the ''[[Wikipedia:Requests|Request departments]]'' page that lists the alternative ways of getting help as seen below. **Place '''{{tl|Help me}}''' (including the curly brackets) "then your question" on [[Special:MyTalk|your talk page]], a volunteer will visit you there! **If you require personal ''[[Wikipedia:Administrators|administrator]]'' assistance in regards to blocking, deleting, protecting, personal harassment or legal threats you can place '''{{tl|Admin help}}''' (including the curly brackets) "then your concerns" on [[Special:MyTalk|your talk page]], an administrator will visit you there! **[[Wikipedia:Adopt-a-User|Adopt-a-User]] – is where you can find experienced Wikipedians that "adopt" new users and mentor them. **[[Wikipedia:Co-op|Co-op]] – a mentorship space where you can work with an experienced Wikipedian to learn about and improve Wikipedia. **Join the [[WP:IRCHELP|#wikipedia-en-help]] IRC channel for real-time chat. **[[Wikipedia:Contact us|Contact Wikipedia]] – is a page that describes how and where to contact Wikipedia directly for a variety of reasons. ===Community standards and advice=== {{Back to contents}} {{anchor|Wikipedia:Help index#Community standards and advice}} *'''[[Wikipedia:Policies and guidelines|Policies & guidelines]]''' – describes how policies and guidelines should normally be developed and maintained. **[[Wikipedia:List of policies and guidelines|List of policies and guidelines]] – lists the main community standards for "English Wikipedia". ***[[Wikipedia:List of policies|List of policies]] – a comprehensive descriptive list of policies. ***[[Wikipedia:List of guidelines|List of guidelines]] – a comprehensive descriptive list of guidelines. ****[[Wikipedia:Simplified ruleset|Simplified rule-set]] – essay about some basic aspect of Wikipedia norms and practices. ****[[Wikipedia:Expectations and norms of the Wikipedia community|Expectations & norms of the community]] – essay about some social norms that editors are expected to follow. ****[[Wikipedia:8 simple rules for editing our encyclopedia|Eight rules for editing]] – essay stating if you start out by following these simple rules, the rest should come naturally. ****[[Wikipedia:Ten Simple Rules for Editing Wikipedia|Ten rules for editing]] – essay that provides tips to make editing smoother. *'''[[WP:INFOPAGES|How-to and information pages]]''' – about pages that contain technical and factual information or supplement guidelines and policies in greater detail. *'''[[WP:Essays|Essays]]''' – Although essays are not policy or guidelines many are worthy of considerations. **[[Wikipedia:WikiProject Council/Guide#Advice_pages|Advice pages]] – guideline about advice pages written by WikiProjects. ***[[Wikipedia:The difference between policies, guidelines and essays|Difference between policies, guidelines & essays]] – essay about what is called a "policy", "guideline" or "essay". ***[[Wikipedia:Essays are not policy|Essays are not policy]] – essay about how it is not a good idea to quote essays as if they were community standards. ***[[Wikipedia:The value of essays|The value of essays]] – essays are not policy or guidelines, but many are worthy of consideration. ****[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell|Essays in a nutshell]] – summarizes the gist of user written essays on Wikipedia. *'''[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Accessibility|Accessibility]]''' – guideline primarily intended to assist those with disabilities, it can be helpful for all readers. **[[Wikipedia:Accessibility dos and don'ts|Accessibility dos and don'ts]] – regardless of disability, all should be able to read, navigate, and contribute easily. **[[Wikipedia:Alternative text for images|Alternative text for images]] – allows the content and function of an image to be understood by text-only readers. *'''[[Wikipedia:Deletion policy|Article deletion]]''' – policy about how articles and other Wikipedia pages can be removed from general view. **[[Wikipedia:How to delete a page|How to delete a page]] – essay with an easy explanation of how to ask for an article to be deleted. **[[Wikipedia:Guide to deletion|Guide to deletion]] – essay with step by step instructions for nominating articles for deletions. **[[Wikipedia:Arguments to avoid in deletion discussions|Arguments to avoid during deletions]] – essay about what not to say or talk about during a deletion. **[[Wikipedia:How to save an article proposed for deletion|Save an article proposed for deletion]] – essay about how to familiarize yourself with the deletion process. **[[Wikipedia:Why was the page I created deleted?|Why was the page I created deleted?]] – essay about the reasons and the ''[[WP:AFTERDELETE|"several" options available]]'' to you. ***[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Deletion|Deletion essays]] – summarizes the gist of user written essays about deletions. *'''[[Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons|Biographies of living persons]]''' (BLP) – guideline about how bios on living persons must be written with great care. **[[Wikipedia:Libel|Libel]] – policy about how it is the responsibility of all to ensure that material posted is not ''[[Defamation|defamatory]]''. ***[[Wikipedia:Avoiding harm|Avoiding harm]] – essay that contains the ideas behind the philosophy that formed the ''BLP''. *'''[[Wikipedia:Blocking policy|Blocking]]''' – policy behind how administrators technically prevent users from editing. **[[Wikipedia:Guide to appealing blocks|Appealing blocks]] – essay about how to appeal a block. **[[Wikipedia:Unblock Ticket Request System|Unblock request]] – you can use the {{t|unblock}} template on your [[Special:MyTalk|talk page]] to request an unblock – however if you have had talk page access removed see ''[[Wikipedia:Unblock Ticket Request System|Unblock request]]''. *'''[[Wikipedia:Child protection|Child protection]]''' – policy about the behavior and actions of adult editors with regards to children. **[[Wikipedia:Offensive material|Offensive material]] – guideline about how articles may contain offensive words and images, but only for a good reason. ***[[Wikipedia:Protecting children's privacy|Protecting children's privacy]] – essay about how all users, including children, are permitted to edit without disclosing information about themselves. ***[[Wikipedia:Guidance for younger editors|Guidance for younger editors]] – essay on advice for young editors about what they should be aware of. *'''[[Wikipedia:Citing sources|Citing sources]]''' – guideline that contains information on how to place and format citations (references). **[[Wikipedia:Verification methods|Verification methods]] – essay about several common methods that Wikipedia editors use to make their articles verifiable. **[[Wikipedia:Referencing for beginners|Referencing for beginners]] – essay that shows you how to use the most popular system for providing inline citations. *'''[[Wikipedia:Conflict of interest|Conflict of interest]]''' – guideline about how it is best to not edit Wikipedia to promote your own interests. **[[Wikipedia:Plain and simple conflict of interest guide|Conflict of interest guide]] – essay for editors who want to write and edit articles about a subject they are affiliated with. **[[Wikipedia:Best practices for editors with close associations|Best practices for editors with close associations]] – essay about having a close association with a topic. **[[Wikipedia:Paid editing (essay)|Paid editing]] – essay that provides advice on what to do, when it comes to Paid Editing & Wikipedia. *'''[[Wikipedia:Consensus|Consensus]]''' – the primary way decisions are made, its accepted as the best method to achieve ''[[Wikipedia:Goal|our goals]]''. **[[Wikipedia:BOLD, revert, discuss cycle|BOLD, revert, discuss cycle]] (BRD) – is a method for reaching consensus. ***[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Consensus and discussion|Consensus and discussion essays]] – summarizes the gist of user written essays on consensus. *'''[[Wikipedia:Copyrights|Copyrights]]''' – policy that states permission is granted to copy, distribute or modify text under ''[[Wikipedia:Text of Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported License|CC BY-SA 3.0]]'' **[[Wikipedia:Close paraphrasing|Close paraphrasing]] – essay that states all should summarize in their own words, instead of closely paraphrasing. **[[Wikipedia:Donating copyrighted materials|Donating published work]] – essay for editors who would like to grant permission to use their own previously published work. **[[Wikipedia:Spotting possible copyright violations|Spotting possible copyright violations]] – essay about spotting copyright violations that are ''[[Wikipedia:Copy-paste|copy-and-pastes]]''. **[[Wikipedia:Requesting copyright permission|Requesting copyright permission]] – essay about editors who would like to get permission to use other people's work. *'''[[Wikipedia:Editing policy|Editing policy]]''' – ''[[Wikipedia:Be bold in updating pages|Be bold in updating articles]]'' in a ''[[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|fair]]'' and ''[[Wikipedia:Verifiability|accurate manner]]'', because [[WP:Editing policy#Wikipedia is a work in progress: perfection is not required|''perfection is not required'']]. **[[WP:DEVELOP|Article development]] – lists the ways in which you can help an article grow. ***[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Article writing|Article writing essays]] – summarizes the gist of user written essays on article building. **[[Wikipedia:Edit warring|Edit warring]] – don't use edits to fight with other editors – there is a ''[[bright line]]'' known as the ''[[#The three-revert rule|three-revert rule]]'' (3RR). **[[Wikipedia:Disruptive editing|Disruptive editing]] – disruptive editors may be ''[[Wikipedia:Blocking policy|blocked]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Banning policy|banned]]'' indefinitely. ***[[Wikipedia:Editorial discretion|Editorial discretion]] – essay about the proper inclusion of relevant and well-sourced content. *'''[[Wikipedia:Etiquette|Etiquette]]''' – principles of decorum, also referred to as "Wikiquette", how to work with others on Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Civility|Civility]] – Wikipedia's basic conduct expectations. **[[Wikipedia:Assume good faith|Assume good faith]] – unless there is clear evidence to the contrary, assume people are trying to help not harm Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:No personal attacks|No personal attacks]] – comment on '''content''', not on the contributors. **[[Wikipedia:Harassment|Harassment]] – ''[[WP:HUSH|unwanted contact]]'', ''[[Wikipedia:WikiBullying|intimidation]]'', ''[[Wikipedia:No legal threats|threats]]'' or ''[[WP:OUTING|posting personal information]]'' is ''[[Wikipedia:No personal attacks#Consequences of personal attacks|strictly prohibited]]''. **[[Wikipedia:Please do not bite the newcomers|Don't bite the newcomers]] – it's very unlikely for a newcomer to be familiar with the community standards. ***[[Wikipedia:IPs are human too|IPs are human too]] – essay about how unregistered users input is just as important in building consensus. ***[[Wikipedia:Honesty|Honesty]] – essay about how truthfulness is expected in all processes, including content discussion. ****[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Civility|Civility essays]] – list of essays that summarizes the gist of user written essays on civility. *'''[[Wikipedia:Ignore all rules|Ignore all rules]]''' (IAR) – policy that states if a ''[[Wikipedia:Policies and guidelines|rule]]'' prevents you from improving or maintaining Wikipedia, '''ignore it'''. **[[Wikipedia:The rules are principles|The rules are principles]] – essay on how policies & guidelines exist as rough approximations of their underlying principles. **[[Wikipedia:Reasonability Rule|Reasonability Rule]] – essay about how anything unreasonable that is being done shouldn't be done at all. **[[Wikipedia:What "Ignore all rules" means|What "Ignore all rules" means]] – essay on how it is acceptable to use ''[[WP:COMMON|common sense]]'' as you go about editing. *'''[[Wikipedia:Image use policy|Image use policy]]''' – policies towards images, like content and copyright issues—applicable to "English Wikipedia". **[[Wikipedia:Guide to image deletion|Image deletion]] – a how to guide for nominating images for deletion.{{Back to contents}} **[[Wikipedia:Image dos and don'ts|Image dos and don'ts]] – images can make Wikipedia more informative, however{{nbsp}}...
{| style="background:transparent; width:50%; " |- ! colspan="2" style="background-color:transparent; font-size:125%" | bgcolor=transparent type=fulltext prefix=Wikipedia:Manual of Style break=yes width=25 searchbuttonlabel=Search placeholder=The Manual of Style |}
* '''[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style|Manual of Style]]''' (MOS) – main guideline page that describes communal consensus on layouts and presentation. **[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Contents|Manual of Style contents]] – descriptive directory of all the pages which make up the Manual of Style. ***[[Wikipedia:Simplified Manual of Style|Simplified Manual of Style]] – essay about the basics commonly used style guidelines. ***[[Wikipedia:Styletips|Styletips]] – a list of advice pages for editors on writing style and formatting. ***[[Wikipedia:Writing better articles|Better articles]] – essay about guidance on how to make articles better. ***[[Wikipedia:The perfect article|Perfect article]] – essay with a point by point guidance on what makes a great article. *'''[[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view|Neutrality]]''' (NPOV) – policy about how articles should represent the views of main scholars and specialists on topics. **[[Wikipedia:NPOV tutorial|NPOV tutorial]] – essay on how to realize you may have a bias you're not aware of. **[[Wikipedia:Be neutral in form|Be neutral in form]] – essay about how some editing methods may lead to disputes over points of views. **[[Wikipedia:Describing points of view|Describing points of view]] – essays that describes the best way to handle a neutral point of view. ***[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Neutral point of view|Neutral point of view essays]] – summarizes the gist of essays on NPOV. *'''[[Wikipedia:No original research|No original research]]''' (OR) – policy about how all material must be attributable to a reliable, published source. **[[Wikipedia:No original research/Examples|Examples of original research]] – some examples of original research. *'''[[Wikipedia:Notability|Notability]]''' – guideline that outlines how suitable a topic may be for its own article or list. **[[Wikipedia:Fringe theories|Fringe theories]] – guideline about how articles should not make a fringe theory appear more notable than it is. ***[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Notability|Notability essays]] – list of essays that summarizes the gist of user written essays on notability. *'''[[Wikipedia:User access levels|User rights]]''' – the ability to perform certain actions in Wikipedia depends on an editor's user access level. **[[Wikipedia:On privacy, confidentiality and discretion|On privacy, confidentiality and discretion]] – essay about how your rights to privacy may not extend as far as you believe. *'''[[Wikipedia:Verifiability|Verifiability]]''' (RS) – policy stating how readers '''must''' be able to check that articles are not fabricated or embellished. **[[Wikipedia:Identifying reliable sources|Identifying reliable sources]] – guideline that discusses how to identify reliable references. ***[[Wikipedia:Cherrypicking|Cherrypicking]] – essay about how to include contradictory and significant qualifying information from the same source. ***[[Wikipedia:Common knowledge|Common knowledge]] – essay about how often people don't actually know what you consider basic knowledge. ***[[Wikipedia:Independent sources|Independent sources]] – essay that gives the opinion of some editors on why independent sourcing is required. ***[[Wikipedia:Potentially unreliable sources|Potentially unreliable sources]] – essay that gives general advice on what is and isn't a reliable source. ****[[Wikipedia:Essays in a nutshell/Verifiability and reliable sources|Verifiability and reliable sources essays]] – summarizes the gist of user written essays on good references. * '''[[Wikipedia:Make technical articles understandable|Understandability]]''' – guideline about how all should strive to make each part of every article as understandable as possible to the widest audience of reader. * '''[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Words to watch|Words to watch]]''' – guideline about how certain expressions should be used with care. * '''[[Wikipedia:Vandalism|Vandalism]]''' – if you see vandalism in an article, the simplest thing to do is just to remove it. {{Back to contents}} ===Directories=== *'''[[Wikipedia:Directory|Directory]]''' – the main list of "Wikipedia" and "Help" namespace directories and indexes * [[Wikipedia:Wikipedia abbreviations|Abbreviations]] – a list of all the abbreviations used on Wikipedia * [[Wikipedia:Department directory|Departments]] – a list of all the different divisions of Wikipedia. * [[Wikipedia:Editor's index to Wikipedia|Editor's index]] – a list of all the pages to help people who edit pages. * [[Wikipedia:Essay directory|Essays]] – a list of pages that contain advice or opinions from one or more Wikipedia contributors. * [[Wikipedia:FAQ Index|FAQ]] – a list of frequently asked questions by topic. * [[Wikipedia:Glossary|Glossary]] – a list of terms (slang) commonly used by editors. * '''[[Wikipedia:List of guidelines|Guidelines]]''' – a descriptive list of official guidelines for "English Wikipedia" * '''[[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Contents|Manual of Style]]''' – a descriptive list of the pages which make up the Manual of Style. * '''[[Wikipedia:List of policies|Policies]]''' – a descriptive list of official policies for "English Wikipedia" * [[Wikipedia:Quick directory|Quick directory]] – a small list of key pages with emphasis on interaction between members of the community. * [[Wikipedia:List of shortcuts|Shortcuts]] – a list of abbreviated redirects and the pages they lead to. * [[Wikipedia:Tips|Tips]] – a list of "tips" created by users at ''[[Wikipedia:Tip of the day|Tip of the day project]]''. ====Help contents by topic ==== {{Back to contents}} *'''[[Help:Menu|Help menu]]''' – main menu-style help page (old format used on the ''[[Help:Contents]]'' page). **[[Help:Menu/Navigating Wikipedia|Navigating Wikipedia]] – a list of help pages for reading the encyclopedia and navigating the site. **[[Help:Menu/Joining Wikipedia|Joining Wikipedia]] – a list of basic introductions about how to get started. **[[Help:Menu/Editing Wikipedia|Editing Wikipedia]] – a list of general help pages for editors. **[[Help:Menu/Links|Links and references]] – a list of page to help with creating links or dealing with references. **[[Help:Menu/Images and media|Images and media]] – a list of pages dealing with using images, videos and sound files. **[[Help:Menu/Tracking changes|Tracking changes]] – a list of pages about tracking the evolution of a page or how to follow a user. **[[Help:Menu/Policies and guidelines|Policies and guidelines]] – a list of community standards. **[[Help:Menu/Communication|Communication]] – a list of pages about contacting another user or keeping yourself informed. **[[Help:Menu/The Wikipedia community|The Wikipedia community]] – a list of pages about resources for editors. **[[Help:Menu/Resources and lists|Resources and lists]] – a list of pages about tips and tools for registered users. **[[Help:Menu/Account settings and maintenance|Account settings and maintenance]] – a list of pages about tips and tools for registered users **[[Help:Menu/Technical information|Technical information]] – a list of pages about tools for "advanced users" and troubleshooting information. *[[Help:Menu/Site map|Site map]] – the "big page" of all the help pages from above. ---- ===Further reading (external links)=== {{Back to contents}} {{commons cat|Instructional videos on using Wikipedia}} *[https://outreach.wikimedia.org/wiki/Bookshelf The Bookshelf] - A vast collection of high-quality, freely licensed, user-generated informational material about Wikipedia * [[wmf:Mission|Mission statement]] – The [[Wikimedia Foundation]] * [[wmf:Values|Wikimedia values]] – The six values of the Wikimedia Foundation * [[wmf:Frequently asked questions|In a nutshell, what is Wikipedia? New to Wikipedia? See our [[Wikipedia:Contributing to Wikipedia|introduction for aspiring contributors]]. If you require interactive assistance, see [[Wikipedia:Questions|asking for help]]. [[Draft:Sandbox|sandbox]] to experiment with page editing.|imageright=[[File:Sandbox.svg|52px]]}} *'''[[Wikipedia:About|About Wikipedia]]''' – general introduction for visitors to Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Administration|Administration]] – discuses both the non-human administrative structure of Wikipedia, as well as its human components. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Overview|FAQ]] – the most common questions about Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Readers|Readers' FAQ]] – concerns and questions readers may have. *[[Wikipedia:Advice for parents|Advice for parents]] – information for parents and legal guardians. * '''[[Wikipedia:Contact us|Contact Wikipedia]]''' – how to get in touch with Wikipedia directly. *'''[[Wikipedia:General disclaimer|General disclaimer]]''' – there is no guarantee of validity and reliability. **[[Wikipedia:Content disclaimer|Content disclaimer]] – Wikipedia is not censored. **[[Wikipedia:Legal disclaimer|Legal disclaimer]] – Wikipedia does not give legal opinions. **[[Wikipedia:Medical disclaimer|Medical disclaimer]] – Wikipedia does not give medical advice. **[[Wikipedia:Risk disclaimer|Risk disclaimer]] – use Wikipedia at your own risk. *'''[[Wikipedia:Policies and guidelines|Policies & guidelines]]''' – community standards set forth by ''[[Wikipedia community|Wikipedians]]''. *'''[[Wikipedia:Principles|Principles]]''' – a listing of fundamental principles by which Wikipedia operates. **[[User:Jimbo Wales/Statement of principles|Statement of principles]] – by founder ''[[Jimmy Wales]]'' and Wikipedians. **[[Wikipedia:Wikipedia in brief|Wikipedia in brief]] – Wikipedia aims to be neutral, verifiable and factual. *[[Wikipedia:Researching with Wikipedia|Researching]] – Wikipedia can be a great tool for learning, however{{nbsp}}... *[[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not|What Wikipedia is not]] – there are certain things that Wikipedia is not. *[[Wikipedia:Questions|Where to ask questions]] – find the right place to ask a question. *[[Wikipedia:Requests|Where to make requests]] – find the right place to make a request. *[[Wikipedia:Who writes Wikipedia|Who writes Wikipedia]] – {{underline|You do!}} {{mono|Just do it!}} {{strong|{{em|[[Wikipedia:Be bold|Be bold!]]}}}} ===Contributor information=== {{Portal|Help}} *'''[[Wikipedia:Contributing to Wikipedia|Contributing to Wikipedia]]''' – the main page that provides information, links, videos and other resources on the basics needed to comprehend, comment on, and contribute to Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Core content policies|Core content policies]] – Wikipedia's content is governed by three principal core content policies. **[[Wikipedia:Simplified ruleset|Simplified rule-set]] – some basic aspect of Wikipedia norms and practices. **[[Wikipedia:Simplified Manual of Style|Simplified Manual of Style]] – the basics about commonly used style guidelines. *'''[[Wikipedia:Welcoming committee/Welcome to Wikipedia|Welcome to Wikipedia]]''' – portal style page to get you started. **[[Help:Getting started|Getting started]] – small listing of pages dealing with the basics. **[[Wikipedia:New contributors' help page|New contributors' help page]] – what would you like to do? **[[Wikipedia:Avoiding common mistakes|Common mistakes]] – a few common mistakes {{mono|''y'all''}} should try to avoid. **[[Wikipedia:Plain and simple|Plain and simple overview]] – all about this amazing project Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:A Primer for newcomers|Primer for newcomers]] – blunt introduction intended to help newcomers. **[[Wikipedia:Learning the ropes|Learning the ropes]] – highlights the resources to help you help Wikipedia. **[[Wikipedia:Help hub|Help hub]] – a page that has links to useful directories. *'''[[Wikipedia:Why create an account?|Why create an account]]''' – don't need to be registered to edit, however it does provide additional features. **[[Wikipedia:Username policy|Choosing a username]] – do not choose names which may be offensive, misleading, disruptive, or promotional. **[[Help:Logging in|How to log in]] – If you are not logged in your edits are labelled in page history with your IP address. *'''[[Wikipedia:Your first article|Your first article]]''' – guide to starting your first encyclopedia article. **[[Wikipedia:Annotated article|Annotated article]] – is a well-constructed sample article, with annotations. ***'''[[Wikipedia:Article wizard|Article wizard]]''' – will walk you through the process of submitting a new article. ****[[Wikipedia:Creation and usage of media files|Creation and usage of media files]] – only logged in users can upload files. *****[[Wikipedia:File Upload Wizard|Upload Wizard]] – will walk you through the process of submitting media. *'''[[Wikipedia:FAQ|Frequently Asked Questions]]''' – common questions about using and contributing. **[[Wikipedia:Editorial oversight and control|Editorial oversight and control]]- we have tens of thousands of editors, from expert scholars to casual readers. **[[Wikipedia:How to help|How to help]] – what anyone can do to contribute. *[[Wikipedia:Instructional material|More instructional material]] – provides links to instructional material useful for users. **[[Wikipedia:Trifecta|Trifecta]] – ultra fast overview of foundational principles related to policies and guidelines. *[[Wikipedia:Ten things you may not know about Wikipedia|Things you may not know about Wikipedia]] – insights specifically targeted at people who have limited experience. *'''[[Wikipedia:Tip of the day|Tip of the day]]''' – provides "very useful" advice daily on how to use or develop Wikipedia more effectively. **See also '''[[Wikipedia:Tips]]''', the complete library of tips arranged by subject. * [[Wikipedia:User page design center|User page design center]] – where you will find all the resources for developing your user page. '''Enjoy!''' {{Back to contents}} ===Frequently asked questions===

*'''[[Wikipedia:FAQ index|FAQ main page]]''' – questions about using and contributing. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Administration|Administration]] – answers some questions related to Administrators. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Article subjects|Article subjects]] – what to do about a specific articles. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Categories|Categories]] – about using Wikipedia's categories. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Contributing|Contributing]] – answers to questions commonly asked by contributors. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Copyright|Copyright]] – four most commonly asked questions about copyright. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Editing|Editing]] – answers the most common questions about editing. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Forking|Forking]] – how do I download and use Wikipedia content. **[[Wikipedia:IRC|IRC (live chat)]] – about "chat rooms" – real-time discussions. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Organizations|Organizations]] – editing without displaying a conflict of interest. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Problems|Problems]] – solving problems you may encounter when browsing or editing. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Readers|Readers]] – addresses concerns and questions readers may have. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Schools|Schools]] – questions teachers, librarians and administrators might have. **[[Wikipedia:FAQ/Technical|Technical]] – answers some questions related to the technical workings. **([[Wikipedia:FAQ/Miscellaneous|Miscellaneous]]) – questions that do not fit into any of the others above. ===How to pages===

tab. **[[Wikipedia:Tutorial/Formatting|Formatting]] – the basics on coding. **[[Wikipedia:Tutorial/Wikipedia links|Links]] – the basics on internal links. **[[Wikipedia:Tutorial/Citing
